attention1.ptxt: Do not use your mouth to apply vacuum to EVAP canister purge valve. Fuel vapor is harmful.

attention2.ptxt: Do not apply vacuum more than –66 kPa (–0.67 kgf/cm2, –9.57 psi, –0.66 bar); otherwise EVAP canister purge valve could be damaged.

s1.ptxt: Prepare to operate EVAP canister purge valve as follows.

item.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1) with ignition “OFF”.
Set ignition “ON”, clear DTC and select “Engine / Active Test” mode on SUZUKI scan tool.

attention3.ptxt: EVAP canister purge valve is operated using scan tool as follows.

item.ptxt: EVAP canister purge valve is OFF: 0%
EVAP canister purge valve is ON: 100%
Connect service wire (2) between EVAP canister purge valve and EVAP canister purge valve connector with ignition “OFF”.

attention3.ptxt: EVAP canister purge valve (1) is operated using service wire as follows.

item.ptxt: EVAP canister purge valve is OFF:
Disconnect “BLU/BLK” harness side of EVAP canister purge valve and ground [a]
EVAP canister purge valve is ON:
Connect “BLU/BLK” harness side of EVAP canister purge valve and ground [b]

s1.ptxt: Disconnect vacuum hose (2) from intake manifold and purge hose from EVAP canister purge valve (1).
Install special tool to vacuum hose.
Check EVAP canister purge valve operation as follows.

s2.ptxt: Check that vacuum of –60 kPa (–0.6 kgf/cm2, –8.7 psi, –0.6 bar) can be applied when EVAP canister purge valve is turned OFF [A].
Check that no vacuum can be applied when EVAP canister purge valve is turned ON [B].

s1.ptxt: If faulty condition is found, check the following.
